title: Wave Grid, FLIP, Odometer, Displacement, Steps
description: Five live UI demos: CSS sine-wave grid, FLIP filter layout, digit odometers, SVG displacement hover, and animated progress steps.

drops.title: Sine Wave Grid
drops.description: CSS animation-delay stagger creates a living sine ripple across a grid of colored cells.
drops.concept: Each cell gets a --d custom property (column + row offset) that drives animation-delay, making the scaleY + hue keyframe appear as a flowing sine wave. Speed, wave scale, and hue are all tunable via sliders.

interaction.mouse: Adjust sliders for speed / wave / hue

drops.title: FLIP Filter Gallery
drops.description: Cards filter by category and fly to new positions using the FLIP animation technique.
drops.concept: FLIP (First, Last, Invert, Play): snapshot element rects before and after DOM change, apply inverse transform, then remove it on rAF so the browser transitions from old to new position. Zero layout thrash, silky smooth.
drops.source: Adam Argyle / Google Chrome Labs FLIP explainer

interaction.click: Category buttons filter cards with spring animation

drops.title: Odometer Counters
drops.description: Digits roll like a physical slot machine using CSS translateY on stacked columns of 0–9.
drops.concept: Each digit is a vertical column of spans (9 through 0), and translateY positions the correct digit in view. CSS cubic-bezier with overshoot creates the physical bounce. font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums prevents layout shift during the roll.

interaction.click: Click stat to roll it; Roll / Reset buttons control all

drops.title: SVG Displacement Hover
drops.description: feTurbulence + feDisplacementMap distort elements as the cursor approaches, creating a ripple/melt effect.
drops.concept: SVG filter feTurbulence generates a noise field; feDisplacementMap uses it to warp pixel positions. As cursor proximity increases, the displacement scale and turbulence baseFrequency animate smoothly — no CSS properties involved, purely SVG filter primitives driven by JS.

interaction.mouse: Hover cards to trigger displacement distortion effect

drops.title: Animated Progress Steps
drops.description: Three linked steppers advance in sync with smooth dot, line, and panel transitions.
drops.concept: Steps use CSS class toggling (active/done) with CSS transitions on border, background, and box-shadow. The connector line between steps uses a ::after pseudo-element that transitions background-color when the preceding step gets .done. Three steppers with different accent colors progress proportionally.
drops.source: Stephanie Eckles / ModernCSS stepper component patterns

interaction.click: Continue/Back buttons or click steps directly to jump
